string.text: Late-game A-Life accumulates too many active entities. AlifeGuard governs the online population with squad-aware culling, round-robin fairness across factions, and hysteresis to prevent oscillation. Squads survive, smart terrains stay consistent.
Limit active entities around you. Farthest go offline first. Traders, companions, quest targets protected.
How many stalkers and mutants can be active simultaneously. Lower = better framerate in dense areas.
Cull target is max minus buffer. Prevents oscillation: cleanup runs less often because population must rise back above max before the next cycle triggers. 0 disables.
[seconds]\nHow often to check entity counts. Lower = more responsive but slight performance cost. 30 is balanced.
NPCs involved in active tasks cannot be removed. Disable if the Zone should be indifferent to your objectives.
Cull distant entities first. If disabled, random selection.
Thin squad members before touching commanders. Keeps squads alive, prevents respawn churn. If disabled, all entities released individually like v1.0.
Spread removals evenly across factions and mutant types. If disabled, farthest entities removed regardless of faction.
Show cleanup statistics via PDA when entities are culled.
Debug and development tools. DEBUG enables structured tracing with trace IDs, per-phase timing, and per-entity release logs. Written to alifeguard.log. Has performance cost.
Controls log verbosity. ERROR: crashes only. WARN: anomalies. INFO: cycle summaries and config. DEBUG: full tracing with timing per entity.

Clamps corrupted already_spawned counters on smart terrains. Negative or non-numeric values cause save crashes (u8 write at smart_terrain.script:955) and infinite spawn loops (max > num always true). Common when running per-NPC despawners that race with the engine's on_unregister decrement. Sanitize runs once on game/level load (before alife pump) and periodically during play. Safe to leave on. Default: on.

Real-time seconds between periodic sanitize passes. Sub-millisecond cost regardless of interval (pure Lua table walk over SIMBOARD.smarts). 300s is a sensible default; lower it only if you observe rapid corruption from another mod. Default: 300.
Run one immediate sanitize pass and report scan count + fix count via PDA. Use to verify the sanitizer fires when expected, or to clean state right before a save.
Display current entity counts and protection statistics via PDA.
Immediately cull excess entities. Use if framerate drops unexpectedly.
Remove all common (respawnable) squads. Story and quest squads kept. Smart terrains repopulate naturally.
Remove ALL squads including story squads. May break active quests. Use as last resort.
